heading: Interference with wildfire suppression, law enforcement, or emergency response effort by operation of unmanned aircraft
label: 46320 Interference with wildfire suppression, law enforcement, or emergency response effort by operation of unmanned aircraft

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), an individual who operates an unmanned aircraft and in so doing knowingly or recklessly interferes with a wildfire suppression, law enforcement, or emergency response effort is liable to the United States Government for a civil penalty of not more than $20,000.

children.heading: .—Exceptions
children.label: b .—Exceptions
children.text: This section does not apply to the operation of an unmanned aircraft conducted by a unit or agency of the United States Government or of a State, tribal, or local government (including any individual conducting such operation pursuant to a contract or other agreement entered into with the unit or agency) for the purpose of protecting the public safety and welfare, including firefighting, law enforcement, or emergency response.

children.heading: .—Compromise
children.label: 1 .—Compromise
children.text: The United States Government may compromise the amount of a civil penalty imposed under this section.

children.heading: .—Setoff
children.label: 2 .—Setoff
children.text: The United States Government may deduct the amount of a civil penalty imposed or compromised under this section from the amounts the Government owes the person liable for the penalty.

children.heading: .—Wildfire
children.label: 1 .—Wildfire
children.text: The term “wildfire” has the meaning given that term in section 2 of the Emergency Wildfire Suppression Act ().42 U.S.C. 1856m

children.heading: .—Wildfire suppression
children.label: 2 .—Wildfire suppression
children.text: The term “wildfire suppression” means an effort to contain, extinguish, or suppress a wildfire.
